task_completion.description: Crawl the target website using Screaming Frog SEO Spider.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog SEO Spider GUI is open with 'https://crawler-test.com/' in the URL bar at the top, and the crawl progress bar shows activity or completion.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Canonicals tab or filter to view canonical tag data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Canonicals' tab is selected in the Screaming Frog interface, displaying a list of URLs and their corresponding canonical link elements.

task_completion.description: Export the complete canonical tag data as a CSV file to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export' dialog box saving a CSV file to '~/Documents/SEO/exports/', or a file manager/terminal showing a newly created CSV file in that directory.

task_completion.description: Write a duplicate content audit report containing specific canonical category counts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with the file '~/Documents/SEO/reports/canonical_report.txt'. The text must explicitly include counts for: self-referencing canonicals, canonicalized to different URLs, missing canonicals, and canonical chains/inconsistencies.

task_completion.description: Include prioritized recommendations in the audit report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor showing 'canonical_report.txt' includes a section detailing prioritized recommendations based on the audit findings.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Screaming Frog GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Screaming Frog SEO Spider desktop application interface being used, rather than terminal commands or scripts simulating the crawl.

integrity.description: The agent actually crawled the site rather than hallucinating the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog interface should show a populated list of URLs from crawler-test.com and non-zero crawl metrics, proving data was gathered before writing the report.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to write the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, vim, or mousepad) is visible on screen editing the report, rather than a single terminal command echoing a pre-written block of text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL is https://crawler-test.com/. The report must be saved to ~/Documents/SEO/reports/canonical_report.txt. The website contains 400+ test pages and 50+ canonical tag test pages. The VLM must verify that the report explicitly includes counts for ALL required categories: self-referencing, canonicalized to different URLs, missing canonicals, AND canonical chains (which might be omitted by a careless agent but is strictly required).
